sourceMovie.title: Coraline
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Coraline for the same reason it broke through in 2009: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a family rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Henry Selick keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The animation and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Super Mario Bros. Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Coraline clicked for you, The Super Mario Bros. Movie (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Coraline?
faq.answer: Try The Super Mario Bros. Movie, Puss in Boots: The Last Wish, Rise of the Guardians—each shares DNA with Coraline while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Coraline?
faq.answer: Coraline (2009) is best described as Animation, Family, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Coraline worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
